removedRuntime: URL token absorption and route-query session restoration

authState: The auth store remains anonymous and unavailable until a safe Web-only login lifecycle is verified.
Initialization deletes only enumerated legacy auth keys and never restores them as authority.
Local logout and auth loss clear private Query entries, protected replay, protected handoffs, and active analysis while preserving public local PGN work and public tournament queries.
A private-query HTTP 401 schedules centralized auth-loss cleanup; HTTP 403 remains a permission error and cannot escalate privileges.
No expiry is invented because no accepted session or stable source expiry exists.
uiState: The login route renders a credential-free unavailable explanation.
Product navigation labels the route as authentication information rather than a working login.
ResourceState distinguishes authentication, permission, unavailable, retryable error, loading, and empty states.
Deterministic unavailable errors do not render retry controls and stop display polling.
Protected replay reports the blocked authentication contract without issuing a request.
governance: Git-backed scanners now ignore tracked paths that have been deleted from the working tree, while still failing closed for every file that can ship.
Secret and mock scanners pass after unsafe auth modules are removed.

browserValidation.interaction: Keyboard focus and Enter activation of the visible 返回赛事 link changed the route from /login to /competitions.
browserValidation.legacyRestorationProbe: A controlled obsolete legacy key was deleted on reload and was not restored as a session.
browserValidation.console: No app-breaking error; the only error was the preexisting nonbreaking favicon.ico 404.

residualBlocks: The confirmed upstream still grants no verified cross-origin browser CORS contract.
Password login remains blocked until a non-openid, non-browser-HMAC Web request is verified.
Finished replay remains blocked until an approved guest-token lifecycle is verified.
The repository has no hosting authority that can remove these upstream contract blocks.
